summary: Repair the macOS Xcode build, sync the IBVault target with the on-disk study sources, normalize mixed ShapeStyle foregroundStyle expressions, expand ARIA context specs, reduce runtime stalls caused by repeated ARIA filesystem scans and materials-library reindexing, stabilize ARIA chat runtime behavior on macOS, improve ARIA math formatting, and add interactive focus/study graphs, and polish ARIA response spacing and flashcard formatting, bundle a local MathJax SVG renderer for full TeX support, and strengthen ARIA paragraph/layout rendering, fix ARIA chat scrolling, add persistent ARIA chat sessions with reopen/new-chat flows while preserving memory behavior, and add an ARIA sidebar toggle plus more selective rich-renderer usage to reduce unnecessary WebKit activity.

nodes.summary: SwiftUI app entrypoint that registers SwiftData models, including StudyPlan.
IBVault target includes StudyPlan.swift and the Study views in the project graph and Sources build phase.
Study views use ShapeStyle-erased foregroundStyle branches so the macOS SwiftUI compiler accepts mixed accent colors and hierarchical styles.
Defines IBColors, which are used by the repaired foregroundStyle expressions.
ARIA records study-plan drafts, plan revisions, ARIA chat exchanges, flashcard generation, review completion, and development updates into persistent action specs.
ARIA reuses in-memory indexes for recent action specs and materials filenames instead of rescanning the filesystem for every prompt build.
Materials Library computes collection counts and aggregate size once per view lifecycle and reuses cached directory listings instead of recursively rescanning on every render.
ARIA chat keeps SwiftData ModelContext work on the main actor and defers streaming auto-scroll updates so macOS runtime warnings do not cascade during token streaming.
ARIA chat renders mixed markdown and LaTeX-style equations locally with readable inline and block formatting instead of depending on a remote web view renderer.
Study effectiveness and focus screens expose interactive Charts-based graphs so users can inspect study momentum, method impact, and focus windows directly in the UI.
ARIA chat normalizes streamed assistant text, restores missing sentence spacing, and renders flashcard-style replies with clearer FRONT/BACK structure and richer typography.
ARIA assistant messages use a bundled local MathJax SVG web renderer for complex TeX, multi-line derivations, flashcard layout, and richer paragraph formatting without CDN dependencies.
ARIA supports persistent chat sessions so users can create a new chat, reopen old chats, and keep per-session conversation history while long-term memory remains available.
ARIA chat remains scrollable even when assistant messages use embedded rich TeX rendering, by preventing nested web views from hijacking scroll gestures.
ARIA chat exposes a collapsible chats sidebar so users can minimize and restore the session list without leaving the chat view.

ARIA only uses the WebKit/MathJax renderer for genuinely TeX-heavy assistant replies, leaving normal paragraphs and flashcard prose on native SwiftUI text rendering to cut unnecessary WebContent churn.
